tree.setup: You are negotiating a distribution agreement with a potential business partner. The partner wants exclusive distribution rights for your product in the northern China market. You need to negotiate favorable terms including performance guarantees, termination clauses, and pricing.
tree.setup_zh: 你正在和一位潜在的商业伙伴谈判一份分销协议。对方希望获得你们产品在华北市场的独家分销权。你需要谈判有利的条款，包括业绩保证、终止条款和定价。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们对贵公司的产品非常看好，愿意投入大量资源进行市场推广。但作为前提条件，我们需要华北地区三年的独家分销权。独家权能保证我们的投入有回报，否则我们在推广上的积极性会大打折扣。
turns.text_pinyin: wǒmen duì guì gōngsī de chǎnpǐn fēicháng kànhǎo, yuànyì tóurù dàliàng zīyuán jìnxíng shìchǎng tuīguǎng. dàn zuòwéi qiántí tiáojiàn, wǒmen xūyào huáběi dìqū sān nián de dújiā fēnxiāo quán. dújiā quán néng bǎozhèng wǒmen de tóurù yǒu huíbào, fǒuzé wǒmen zài tuīguǎng shàng de jījí xìng huì dà dǎ zhékòu.
turns.text_en: We're very optimistic about your company's product and willing to invest significant resources in market promotion. However, as a precondition, we need exclusive distribution rights for the northern China region for three years. Exclusivity guarantees our investment will have returns -- otherwise our enthusiasm for promotion would be significantly diminished.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the exclusivity request -- you want to give it conditionally, not unconditionally.

options.text_zh: 感谢陈总的诚意，我们非常重视这次合作的机会。关于独家分销权，我们原则上可以考虑，但需要附加业绩保证条款。坦白讲，我们不能把一个战略性市场完全锁定给一家合作伙伴三年，却没有任何绩效约束。我的建议是：第一年设定一个基础销售额目标，达标后自动续期独家权；如果连续两个季度未能完成目标的百分之七十，我们保留引入其他分销商的权利。这样既保护了你们的投资回报，也保障了我们的市场覆盖效率。这个框架您觉得可以作为讨论的基础吗？
options.text_pinyin: gǎnxiè chén zǒng de chéngyì, wǒmen fēicháng zhòngshì zhè cì hézuò de jīhuì. guānyú dújiā fēnxiāo quán, wǒmen yuánzé shàng kěyǐ kǎolǜ, dàn xūyào fùjiā yèjì bǎozhèng tiáokuǎn. tǎnbái jiǎng, wǒmen bù néng bǎ yī gè zhànlüè xìng shìchǎng wánquán suǒdìng gěi yī jiā hézuò huǒbàn sān nián, què méiyǒu rènhé jìxiào yuēshù. wǒ de jiànyì shì: dì yī nián shèdìng yī gè jīchǔ xiāoshòu'é mùbiāo, dábiāo hòu zìdòng xùqī dújiā quán; rúguǒ liánxù liǎng gè jìdù wèi néng wánchéng mùbiāo de bǎifēnzhī qīshí, wǒmen bǎoliú yǐnrù qítā fēnxiāo shāng de quánlì. zhèyàng jì bǎohù le nǐmen de tóuzī huíbào, yě bǎozhàng le wǒmen de shìchǎng fùgài xiàolǜ. zhège kuàngjià nín juéde kěyǐ zuòwéi tǎolùn de jīchǔ ma?
options.text_en: Thank you Mr. Chen for your sincerity -- we greatly value this partnership opportunity. Regarding exclusive distribution rights, we can consider it in principle, but with performance guarantee clauses attached. Frankly, we can't lock a strategic market entirely to one partner for three years without any performance constraints. My suggestion is: set a baseline sales target for year one, with exclusivity automatically renewed upon achievement; if the target falls below 70% for two consecutive quarters, we reserve the right to introduce additional distributors. This protects both your investment returns and our market coverage efficiency. Could this framework serve as a basis for discussion?
options.feedback: Expert negotiation -- says yes conditionally rather than outright no, frames performance guarantees as mutual protection, and proposes specific trigger mechanisms. Ending with 作为讨论的基础 keeps the door open rather than presenting an ultimatum. 业绩保证条款, 绩效约束, and 市场覆盖效率 are precisely deployed.

options.text_zh: 三年独家权我们给不了。一年可以考虑。
options.text_pinyin: sān nián dújiā quán wǒmen gěi bù liǎo. yī nián kěyǐ kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: We can't give three years of exclusivity. One year we can consider.
options.feedback: Blunt rejection without explanation or counter-proposal. In negotiations, explain why and offer alternatives. A flat 'no' without conditions leaves no room for creative solutions.

options.text_zh: 独家分销权没问题，三年就三年。我们很期待合作。
options.text_pinyin: dújiā fēnxiāo quán méi wèntí, sān nián jiù sān nián. wǒmen hěn qīdài hézuò.
options.text_en: Exclusive distribution is no problem -- three years it is. We're looking forward to the partnership.
options.feedback: Giving away your most valuable bargaining chip without conditions is a major negotiation mistake. Unconditional exclusivity with no performance guarantees means if they underperform, you're trapped for three years.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 业绩保证我们可以接受，这说明贵方也是在认真考虑长期合作的。但百分之七十的门槛有点高——市场开拓初期有很多不确定因素，第一年能达到目标的百分之六十就已经很不容易了。另外，关于产品的采购价格，我们希望能拿到出厂价的七折。量大的话，我们能保证稳定的现金流。
turns.text_pinyin: yèjì bǎozhèng wǒmen kěyǐ jiēshòu, zhè shuōmíng guì fāng yěshì zài rènzhēn kǎolǜ chángqī hézuò de. dàn bǎifēnzhī qīshí de ménkǎn yǒudiǎn gāo——shìchǎng kāituò chūqī yǒu hěn duō bù quèdìng yīnsù, dì yī nián néng dádào mùbiāo de bǎifēnzhī liùshí jiù yǐjīng hěn bù róngyì le. lìngwài, guānyú chǎnpǐn de cǎigòu jiàgé, wǒmen xīwàng néng ná dào chūchǎng jià de qī zhé. liàng dà dehuà, wǒmen néng bǎozhèng wěndìng de xiànjīn liú.
turns.text_en: We can accept performance guarantees -- this shows your side is seriously considering long-term cooperation. But the 70% threshold is a bit high -- there are many uncertain factors in the initial market development phase; reaching 60% of the target in the first year would already be quite challenging. Also, regarding the procurement price, we'd like 30% off the factory price. With large volumes, we can guarantee stable cash flow.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Negotiate on the performance threshold and pricing.

options.text_zh: 关于业绩门槛，我能理解市场开拓期的挑战。这样吧，我们做一个阶梯式的安排：第一年的门槛可以降到目标的百分之六十，但从第二年起恢复到百分之七十。这给了你们充足的市场培育时间。至于价格方面，七折确实低于我们正常的渠道价格体系。考虑到独家合作的战略意义，我们可以给到七五折作为基准价。但如果年度采购量超过约定数量的百分之二十，超出部分我们可以再给一个额外的百分之五的折扣。这样你们有动力把量做上去，我们也有合理的利润空间。价格和业绩挂钩，对双方都公平。
options.text_pinyin: guānyú yèjì ménkǎn, wǒ néng lǐjiě shìchǎng kāituò qī de tiǎozhàn. zhèyàng ba, wǒmen zuò yī gè jiētī shì de ānpái: dì yī nián de ménkǎn kěyǐ jiàng dào mùbiāo de bǎifēnzhī liùshí, dàn cóng dì èr nián qǐ huīfù dào bǎifēnzhī qīshí. zhè gěi le nǐmen chōngzú de shìchǎng péiyù shíjiān. zhìyú jiàgé fāngmiàn, qī zhé quèshí dīyú wǒmen zhèngcháng de qúdào jiàgé tǐxì. kǎolǜ dào dújiā hézuò de zhànlüè yìyì, wǒmen kěyǐ gěi dào qī wǔ zhé zuòwéi jīzhǔn jià. dàn rúguǒ niándù cǎigòu liàng chāoguò yuēdìng shùliàng de bǎifēnzhī èrshí, chāochū bùfèn wǒmen kěyǐ zài gěi yī gè éwài de bǎifēnzhī wǔ de zhékòu. zhèyàng nǐmen yǒu dònglì bǎ liàng zuò shàngqù, wǒmen yě yǒu hélǐ de lìrùn kōngjiān. jiàgé hé yèjì guàgōu, duì shuāngfāng dōu gōngpíng.
options.text_en: Regarding the performance threshold, I understand the challenges of the market development phase. Let's do a graduated arrangement: year one's threshold can drop to 60% of target, but from year two it returns to 70%. This gives you sufficient market cultivation time. As for pricing, 30% off is indeed below our normal channel pricing structure. Considering the strategic significance of exclusive cooperation, we can offer 25% off as the base price. However, if annual procurement exceeds the agreed volume by 20%, we'll provide an additional 5% discount on the excess. This way you're incentivized to grow volume, and we maintain reasonable profit margins. Tying price to performance is fair for both sides.
options.feedback: Masterful negotiation -- concedes on year-one threshold (shows flexibility) but holds year-two at 70% (protects your position). The graduated pricing with volume incentives creates alignment. The phrase 价格和业绩挂钩 neatly frames the structure as mutually beneficial. 阶梯式, 基准价, 渠道价格体系, and 利润空间 demonstrate advanced commercial vocabulary.

options.text_zh: 门槛可以降一点，七五折可以考虑。我们回去讨论一下再给您答复。
options.text_pinyin: ménkǎn kěyǐ jiàng yīdiǎn, qīwǔ zhé kěyǐ kǎolǜ. wǒmen huíqù tǎolùn yīxià zài gěi nín dáfù.
options.text_en: The threshold can be lowered a bit, and 25% off is something we can consider. Let me discuss with my team and get back to you.
options.feedback: Shows willingness to negotiate but delays decision-making. In a negotiation, coming to the table prepared to make conditional offers is more effective than repeatedly needing to consult internally.

options.text_zh: 这两个问题我需要请示领导才能给答复。
options.text_pinyin: zhè liǎng gè wèntí wǒ xūyào qǐngshì lǐngdǎo cái néng gěi dáfù.
options.text_en: I need to consult with my leadership before I can answer on these two points.
options.feedback: Undercuts your authority at the negotiating table. If you're the lead negotiator, you should have authority ranges pre-approved. Deferring on basic terms signals you can't make decisions, weakening your position.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 阶梯式的安排我觉得很合理。七五折作为基准也可以接受，量大再优惠的条件也很有吸引力。不过我还想讨论一个关键条款——合同终止后的客户归属问题。如果合同到期或者提前终止，我们在合作期间开发的客户资源应该归谁？这个问题直接关系到我们前期投入的保障。
turns.text_pinyin: jiētī shì de ānpái wǒ juéde hěn hélǐ. qīwǔ zhé zuòwéi jīzhǔn yě kěyǐ jiēshòu, liàng dà zài yōuhuì de tiáojiàn yě hěn yǒu xīyǐnlì. búguò wǒ hái xiǎng tǎolùn yī gè guānjiàn tiáokuǎn——hétong zhōngzhǐ hòu de kèhù guīshǔ wèntí. rúguǒ hétong dào qī huòzhě tíqián zhōngzhǐ, wǒmen zài hézuò qījiān kāifā de kèhù zīyuán yīnggāi guī shéi? zhège wèntí zhíjiē guānxì dào wǒmen qiánqī tóurù de bǎozhàng.
turns.text_en: The graduated arrangement sounds very reasonable. 25% off as a baseline is also acceptable, and the volume discount is attractive. However, I'd like to discuss one more critical clause -- customer ownership after contract termination. If the contract expires or is terminated early, who do the customer resources we developed during the partnership belong to? This directly relates to the protection of our initial investment.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Address the customer ownership clause for post-termination scenarios.

options.text_zh: 这个问题非常重要，也是分销协议中最容易产生纠纷的条款之一。我的建议是区分两种情况处理。第一种，如果合同正常到期且不续约，鉴于你们在客户开发上的投入，我们同意设置十二个月的保护期——在此期间，你们仍可服务现有客户，我们不会直接接触或转移这些客户关系。保护期结束后，客户有权自由选择渠道。第二种，如果因为业绩未达标导致合同提前终止，保护期缩短为六个月。此外，为了保障双方利益，合同中应加入竞业禁止条款：合同终止后两年内，你们不得代理与我方产品直接竞争的同类产品进入相同市场。这些条款我们都可以请双方的法务团队进一步细化。
options.text_pinyin: zhège wèntí fēicháng zhòngyào, yěshì fēnxiāo xiéyì zhōng zuì róngyì chǎnshēng jiūfēn de tiáokuǎn zhī yī. wǒ de jiànyì shì qūfēn liǎng zhǒng qíngkuàng chǔlǐ. dì yī zhǒng, rúguǒ hétong zhèngcháng dào qī qiě bù xùyuē, jiànyú nǐmen zài kèhù kāifā shàng de tóurù, wǒmen tóngyì shèzhì shí'èr gè yuè de bǎohù qī——zài cǐ qījiān, nǐmen réng kě fúwù xiànyǒu kèhù, wǒmen bú huì zhíjiē jiēchù huò zhuǎnyí zhèxiē kèhù guānxì. bǎohù qī jiéshù hòu, kèhù yǒu quán zìyóu xuǎnzé qúdào. dì èr zhǒng, rúguǒ yīnwèi yèjì wèi dábiāo dǎozhì hétong tíqián zhōngzhǐ, bǎohù qī suōduǎn wéi liù gè yuè. cǐwài, wèile bǎozhàng shuāngfāng lìyì, hétong zhōng yīng jiārù jìngyè jìnzhǐ tiáokuǎn: hétong zhōngzhǐ hòu liǎng nián nèi, nǐmen bùdé dàilǐ yǔ wǒ fāng chǎnpǐn zhíjiē jìngzhēng de tónglèi chǎnpǐn jìnrù xiāngtóng shìchǎng. zhèxiē tiáokuǎn wǒmen dōu kěyǐ qǐng shuāngfāng de fǎwù tuánduì jìnyībù xìhuà.
options.text_en: This is a very important question and one of the most dispute-prone clauses in distribution agreements. My suggestion is to differentiate between two scenarios. First, if the contract expires normally without renewal, given your investment in customer development, we agree to a 12-month protection period -- during which you can continue serving existing customers and we won't directly contact or transfer those customer relationships. After the protection period, customers are free to choose their channel. Second, if the contract is terminated early due to underperformance, the protection period is shortened to 6 months. Additionally, to protect both parties' interests, the contract should include a non-compete clause: for two years after termination, you may not distribute directly competing products in the same market. We can have both sides' legal teams refine these clauses further.
options.feedback: Legally sophisticated -- distinguishes between normal expiration and early termination with different protection periods, protecting the partner's investment while preserving your leverage. Adding the non-compete clause protects your market position. Deferring to legal teams for refinement is a smart closing move. 保护期, 竞业禁止条款, and 法务团队 show command of contract law vocabulary.

options.text_zh: 客户资源当然是双方共有的。合同结束后可以协商解决。
options.text_pinyin: kèhù zīyuán dāngrán shì shuāngfāng gòngyǒu de. hétong jiéshù hòu kěyǐ xiéshāng jiějué.
options.text_en: Customer resources are of course shared by both sides. We can negotiate after the contract ends.
options.feedback: Vague and legally weak. 'We can negotiate later' on a critical clause is how lawsuits start. Define terms now when both parties are cooperative, not after the relationship may have soured.

options.text_zh: 客户是在我们品牌下开发的，合同终止后自然应该归我方。
options.text_pinyin: kèhù shì zài wǒmen pǐnpái xià kāifā de, hétong zhōngzhǐ hòu zìrán yīnggāi guī wǒ fāng.
options.text_en: Customers were developed under our brand, so naturally they should belong to us after termination.
options.feedback: Ignores the partner's investment in customer development, which is a legitimate concern. This position would likely kill the deal. A good negotiator finds structures that acknowledge both sides' contributions.

tree.cultural_note: Business negotiations (商务谈判 shāngwù tánpàn) in China often involve building a framework agreement first, then refining details. Using 原则上 (in principle) signals willingness to negotiate while reserving conditions. The concept of 共赢 (gòng yíng, win-win) is not just rhetoric -- Chinese negotiators value proposals where both sides benefit visibly. Deferring fine points to 法务团队 (legal teams) is standard practice. Key terms include 独家分销权 (exclusive distribution rights), 业绩保证 (performance guarantee), 竞业禁止 (non-compete), and 保护期 (protection period).
